title: Branch 7 Edge Dropped Off the Overlay Overnight

ticket.summary: cedge-br07 disappeared from the SD-WAN overlay at 00:14 last night — vManage shows it offline, all its BFD sessions are gone, and Branch 7 has no WAN connectivity to other sites (local internet breakout still works). No config changes were pushed. The device is reachable via its out-of-band console.

topology.description: cedge-br07 forms DTLS control connections to the vBond orchestrator, vManage, and two vSmart controllers, and data-plane BFD/IPsec tunnels to other sites. Control-plane authentication uses certificates: each edge presents its device certificate and validates the controllers' certificates. Without valid control connections to vSmart, no OMP routes and no data-plane tunnels.

monitoring.notes: Control-connection count for cedge-br07 went 4 -> 0 at exactly 00:14 GMT with local error CRTVERFL (certificate verification failure) on every attempt since. The device certificate's Not-After timestamp is Jul 17 2026 00:14 GMT — the precise minute the connections dropped. No other device in the fleet has a certificate expiring this quarter; br07 was the pilot device enrolled a year earlier than the rest.

hints: All control connections dropped at the same instant with local error CRTVERFL in the connection history — look up what that error code means before suspecting transport or config.
Compare the device certificate validity window ('show sdwan certificate installed') against the exact time the connections dropped and the current clock.

The device certificate expired at Jul 17 00:14 GMT — the exact drop time. CRTVERFL = certificate verification failure. Renew/re-enroll the device certificate (and set expiry alerting) to restore control connections.

solution.root_cause: cedge-br07's device certificate expired at Jul 17 2026 00:14 GMT — the exact moment all DTLS control connections dropped with error CRTVERFL (certificate verification failure). With no valid certificate, the edge cannot authenticate to vBond/vSmart, receives no OMP routes, and all data-plane tunnels are torn down. The device was the pilot unit enrolled a year before the rest of the fleet, so it expired alone.
solution.fix: Renew the device certificate (re-enroll via vManage / the CA used for enrollment) on cedge-br07 through the out-of-band path, confirm control connections re-establish and BFD sessions return, then add certificate-expiry monitoring/alerts for the whole overlay.
